sourceMovie.title: The Spy Next Door
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Spy Next Door (2010) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Brian Levant's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: My Spy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Spy Next Door clicked for you, My Spy (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Cannonball Run

faq.question: What should I watch after The Spy Next Door?
faq.answer: Try My Spy, The Cannonball Run, Toy Story 3—each shares DNA with The Spy Next Door while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Spy Next Door?
faq.answer: The Spy Next Door (2010) is best described as Action, Comedy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Spy Next Door worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
